page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on a KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average tier across most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1578.48 and a multi-core score of 8323.63, supported by an L3 cache of 33 MB. Memory bandwidth tests show 465.63 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 56.48 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves 8.04 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 8.02 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, LLM inference performance is limited, with a poor rating for medium 7B models at 6.26 tokens per second for text generation, while small 135M models run at 136.02 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfc7.2xlarge offers a high-compute, low-memory profile suitable for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-effective choice for raw processing power, but a poor fit for memory-intensive databases or large-scale AI model inference. The lack of bundled local storage means users must attach external block storage, which introduces additional I/O considerations.

This instance is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations. It is also appropriate for single-threaded or multi-threaded decompression tasks, as demonstrated by its bzip3 multi-threaded decompression throughput of 39.32 GB/sec.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average performance across standard compute tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 5101.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 8.04 million operations per second. While highly capable for software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations, its limited memory capacity and lack of hardware acceleration make it poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference or memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, designed for CPU-bound workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc7.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors for CPU-intensive workloads requiring a balanced memory footprint.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-core Intel Xeon 8369HB processor at 3.3 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core resource density)
Delivers 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Achieves 8.04 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and decompression tasks
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU acceleration
